subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV 2025, OECD, World Bank, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Colombian official (DANE, Banco de la Republica) + Colombian/Spanish-language press (El Tiempo, El Espectador, La Silla Vacia, Semana).

Politics: a leftist government gridlocked with Congress and the courts; 2025 brought a US-relations rupture (a decertification and US sanctions on the president) and the assassination of a presidential candidate, with a tense May-2026 election ahead.

Standout drags: fiscal deterioration, internal armed conflict, elevated crime, and US friction; genuine strengths are a credible central bank, sound banks, strong courts, and lawful migration handling. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone; the US-relations framing leans to US outlets.

economic.notes: A credible central bank and sound banks, against a fiscal deterioration and a transition-deterred commodity sector.

fiscal_state.notes: An escape clause invoked in 2025 suspended the fiscal rule through 2027, with a deficit ~7% GDP and net debt above the anchor; elevated sovereign spreads. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: An independent, credible inflation-targeter that proactively hiked - a genuine institutional strength. (Banco de la Republica)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The peso is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Sticky around ~5%, projected to rise before converging to target by 2027 - above target but credibly managed. (BanRep)

banking_stability.notes: Well-capitalised with adequate provisioning, judged resilient. (IMF; BanRep FSR)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Oil/coal/coffee exposure with an energy-transition push that deters hydrocarbon investment, partly offset by strong institutions; weak private investment. (IMF; press)

institutional.notes: Strong courts and credible statistics, against weak rural state presence and a confrontational executive.

rule_of_law.notes: A strong constitutional court and council of state actively check the executive, against shrinking civic space, weak rural state presence, and criminal infiltration. (WJP Rule of Law 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: The statistics agency (DANE) is credible and independent. (DANE)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Mixed - capable central institutions with thin rural delivery. (World Bank; IMF)

press_freedom.notes: A vibrant, pluralistic press (RSF ~115th) with journalists threatened in conflict zones and confrontational government rhetoric. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: Strong liberties and lawful migration handling, against polarised gridlock, political violence, and inequality.

political_stability.notes: A leftist government gridlocked with Congress and the courts amid high polarisation, the 2025 assassination of a presidential candidate, and a tense run-up to the May-2026 vote; institutions hold but are strained. (La Silla Vacia; NPR)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong constitutional protections and active courts. (WJP; V-Dem)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A landmark program regularised ~2.8M Venezuelan migrants on long-term status - relatively open and lawful, though support is quietly eroding. (GFMD)

social_cohesion.notes: High inequality and urban-rural and conflict-legacy divides. (World Bank; ICG)

demographics.notes: Still young, but with fertility falling sharply and ageing/emigration ahead. (DANE; BanRep)

geopolitical.notes: An open economy whose historic US alliance is at a nadir, dominated by internal armed conflict.

alliance_reliability.notes: A historic US ally whose ties hit a nadir in 2025 - a decertification, US sanctions on the president, and deportation/tariff clashes - strained and near-broken. (TIME; Atlantic Council)

conflict_involvement.notes: An internal armed conflict - ELN and FARC-dissident resurgence, a stalling 'total peace' process, and armed groups disrupting the 2026 campaign. (ICG; InsightCrime)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open economy, not under sanctions, with no capital controls. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Improving infrastructure, against multi-hazard climate exposure, a healthcare crisis, and high regional crime.

climate.notes: Most of the population exposed to multiple hazards, with La Nina/El Nino swings and Andean/coastal flood-landslide risk. (GFDRR/World Bank)

healthcare.notes: Broad coverage but an acute system crisis - a rejected reform, declining funding, and worsening access. (El Espectador; Colombia One)

infrastructure.notes: Improving (metro and rail projects) but mountainous, uneven, with rural gaps. (trade.gov)

crime_safety.notes: An elevated and rising homicide rate, extortion, and kidnapping, with conflict zones acutely dangerous against safer major cities. (InsightCrime; Statista)

disaster_insurance.notes: A developing market with sovereign catastrophe instruments advancing, against seismic and flood exposure and low retail penetration. (World Bank; IDF)

summary: A middle-income economy whose genuine institutional strengths - a credible, independent central bank, sound banks, a strong constitutional court, robust civil liberties, and a landmark lawful regularisation of Venezuelan migrants - are offset by a fiscal deterioration (the suspended fiscal rule), an internal armed conflict with resurgent ELN and FARC-dissident groups, elevated crime, a healthcare-system crisis, and a 2025 rupture in the historic US alliance. Politics are polarised and gridlocked, marked by the assassination of a presidential candidate, with tail risks running both ways into the May-2026 election.
